text: ODD + OOD Detection for Safe Autonomy
textPreview: ODD + OOD Detection for Safe Autonomy

text: Notebook synthesis and final recommendation for a camera-based safety gate using BDD100K weather shifts.
textPreview: Notebook synthesis and final recommendation for a camera-based safety gate using BDD100K weather shifts.

text:
- Replaces the proposal-style expected results with the
actual notebook evidence.
- Separates classifier wins from explicit OOD gate wins.
- Keeps the conclusion honest about split differences
across notebooks.
textPreview: - Replaces the proposal-style expected results with the | actual notebook evidence. | - Separates classifier wins from explicit OOD gate wins. | - Keeps the conclusion honest about split differences | across notebooks.

role: metric card: Best supervised val accuracy
metric accent: Best supervised val accuracy

text: Recommended final direction: present ConvNeXt-Tiny as the best classifier, CLIP + kNN as the best OOD gate, and a shared-split rerun as the next milestone.
textPreview: Recommended final direction: present ConvNeXt-Tiny as the best classifier, CLIP + kNN as the best OOD gate, and a shared-split rerun as the next milestone.

text: Detect when the perception stack is inside the operational design domain, and abstain when weather shifts make camera predictions unreliable.
textPreview: Detect when the perception stack is inside the operational design domain, and abstain when weather shifts make camera predictions unreliable.

text:
- Input: one front-camera frame from the perception
stack.
- Output: TRUST, SLOW DOWN, or ABSTAIN.
- Safety goal: cut false-safe cases where OOD frames
are still trusted.
- Deployment goal: keep the gate light enough for
per-frame use.
textPreview: - Input: one front-camera frame from the perception | stack. | - Output: TRUST, SLOW DOWN, or ABSTAIN. | - Safety goal: cut false-safe cases where OOD frames | are still trusted. | - Deployment goal: keep the gate light

text: Original framing adapted from the initial presentation deck.
textPreview: Original framing adapted from the initial presentation deck.

text:
Classifier is confident and the OOD score stays
below threshold.
textPreview: Classifier is confident and the OOD score stays | below threshold.

text:
Prediction is usable, but the margin is narrow
and the OOD score is near threshold.
textPreview: Prediction is usable, but the margin is narrow | and the OOD score is near threshold.

text:
Reject the frame when the representation looks
out-of-domain or uncertainty spikes.
textPreview: Reject the frame when the representation looks | out-of-domain or uncertainty spikes.

text: Primary evaluation metrics across the notebooks: AUROC, AUPR, FPR@95TPR, ECE, false-safe rate, and coverage.
textPreview: Primary evaluation metrics across the notebooks: AUROC, AUPR, FPR@95TPR, ECE, false-safe rate, and coverage.

text: The notebooks are related, but they do not all use the same split. The final story must respect that difference.
textPreview: The notebooks are related, but they do not all use the same split. The final story must respect that difference.

text: Important caveat: the numbers are directionally useful, but not every method was evaluated on the same train/val protocol.
textPreview: Important caveat: the numbers are directionally useful, but not every method was evaluated on the same train/val protocol.

text:
- Train: 16,000 frames (10,000
ID / 6,000 OOD).
- Val: 3,988 frames (2,500 ID
/ 1,488 OOD).
- OOD buckets: rain and
fog/snow.
- Bucketed corpus in the
backbone notebook totals
70,587 images.
textPreview: - Train: 16,000 frames (10,000 | ID / 6,000 OOD). | - Val: 3,988 frames (2,500 ID | / 1,488 OOD). | - OOD buckets: rain and | fog/snow. | - Bucketed corpus in the | backbone notebook totals | 70,587 images.

text:
- Train ID bank: 37,344
clear-weather frames.
- Validation: 5,346 ID and
1,520 OOD frames.
- OOD weather in val: rainy,
snowy, foggy.
- Goal: measure distance from
a clean in-domain feature
bank.
textPreview: - Train ID bank: 37,344 | clear-weather frames. | - Validation: 5,346 ID and | 1,520 OOD frames. | - OOD weather in val: rainy, | snowy, foggy. | - Goal: measure distance from | a clean in-domain feature | bank.

text: Used by Vit+knn, vit_l_14, and Deep_Ensemble_Mahalanobis notebooks
textPreview: Used by Vit+knn, vit_l_14, and Deep_Ensemble_Mahalanobis notebooks

text:
- Train: 61,744 frames.
- Validation: 8,843 frames.
- ID mapping: clear, partly
cloudy, overcast.
- OOD mapping: rainy, snowy,
foggy.
textPreview: - Train: 61,744 frames. | - Validation: 8,843 frames. | - ID mapping: clear, partly | cloudy, overcast. | - OOD mapping: rainy, snowy, | foggy.

text: We tested both supervised classifiers and explicit OOD rejectors, then checked which ideas were strong enough to carry into a final recommendation.
textPreview: We tested both supervised classifiers and explicit OOD rejectors, then checked which ideas were strong enough to carry into a final recommendation.

text:
- Frozen ImageNet features with
a binary head.
- Main baseline for calibration
and uncertainty scoring.
textPreview: - Frozen ImageNet features with | a binary head. | - Main baseline for calibration | and uncertainty scoring.

text:
- MSP, energy, MC dropout, and
ensemble entropy.
- Best AUROC was still only
0.5263.
textPreview: - MSP, energy, MC dropout, and | ensemble entropy. | - Best AUROC was still only | 0.5263.

text:
- Five MLP heads on cached
ResNet features.
- 91.38% validation accuracy on
the full-weather split.
textPreview: - Five MLP heads on cached | ResNet features. | - 91.38% validation accuracy on | the full-weather split.

text:
- One-class and distance
baselines around the ID feature
cloud.
- Mahalanobis beat SVDD, but
both trailed CLIP + kNN.
textPreview: - One-class and distance | baselines around the ID feature | cloud. | - Mahalanobis beat SVDD, but | both trailed CLIP + kNN.

text:
- Distance-to-ID in CLIP
embedding space.
- Strongest OOD detector family
in the notebooks.
textPreview: - Distance-to-ID in CLIP | embedding space. | - Strongest OOD detector family | in the notebooks.

text:
- ResNet50, EfficientNet-B3,
ConvNeXt-Tiny, and CLIP
ViT-B/16.
- ConvNeXt-Tiny was best at
0.9368 validation accuracy.
textPreview: - ResNet50, EfficientNet-B3, | ConvNeXt-Tiny, and CLIP | ViT-B/16. | - ConvNeXt-Tiny was best at | 0.9368 validation accuracy.

text: Pattern across the notebooks: representation quality plus distance-based rejection worked better than confidence scores alone.
textPreview: Pattern across the notebooks: representation quality plus distance-based rejection worked better than confidence scores alone.

text: ResNet baseline: calibrated, but weak at OOD separation
textPreview: ResNet baseline: calibrated, but weak at OOD separation

text: The frozen ResNet-50 classifier learns the task, yet its uncertainty scores still overlap too heavily between ID and OOD.
textPreview: The frozen ResNet-50 classifier learns the task, yet its uncertainty scores still overlap too heavily between ID and OOD.

role: metric card: Abstain rate at 5% FS
metric accent: Abstain rate at 5% FS

text: Calibration improved a lot, but OOD separation stayed weak and the gate had to abstain on almost every validation frame to hit the false-safe target.
textPreview: Calibration improved a lot, but OOD separation stayed weak and the gate had to abstain on almost every validation frame to hit the false-safe target.

role: image panel: ResNet uncertainty method comparison

text: Distance from an in-domain feature bank was much more useful than raw confidence. CLIP + kNN was the strongest explicit rejector in the notebooks.
textPreview: Distance from an in-domain feature bank was much more useful than raw confidence. CLIP + kNN was the strongest explicit rejector in the notebooks.

text:
- AUROC 0.5841 | Cov@5%FS 0.0625.
- Rejected OOD, but over-abstained on ID.
textPreview: - AUROC 0.5841 | Cov@5%FS 0.0625. | - Rejected OOD, but over-abstained on ID.

text:
- AUROC 0.6525 | FPR95 0.8315.
- Best ResNet feature baseline, still below
CLIP.
textPreview: - AUROC 0.6525 | FPR95 0.8315. | - Best ResNet feature baseline, still below | CLIP.

text:
- AUROC 0.7412 | AUPR 0.4366.
- Best explicit OOD gate in the notebooks.
textPreview: - AUROC 0.7412 | AUPR 0.4366. | - Best explicit OOD gate in the notebooks.

text:
- AUROC 0.7326 | Cov@5%FS 0.1936.
- Near-tie with a heavier model.
textPreview: - AUROC 0.7326 | Cov@5%FS 0.1936. | - Near-tie with a heavier model.

text: Recommendation for the OOD gate: CLIP + kNN. ViT-B/32 has the highest AUROC/AUPR, while ViT-L/14 is similar but heavier.
textPreview: Recommendation for the OOD gate: CLIP + kNN. ViT-B/32 has the highest AUROC/AUPR, while ViT-L/14 is similar but heavier.

text: When the task is framed as binary in-ODD vs OOD classification, stronger backbones improved validation accuracy noticeably.
textPreview: When the task is framed as binary in-ODD vs OOD classification, stronger backbones improved validation accuracy noticeably.

text: Bucketed corpus in the backbone notebook: 70,587 total images across in_odd, rain, and fog_snow buckets.
textPreview: Bucketed corpus in the backbone notebook: 70,587 total images across in_odd, rain, and fog_snow buckets.

text: Best val accuracy in backbone sweep
textPreview: Best val accuracy in backbone sweep

text: Strong baseline, but not the top classifier
textPreview: Strong baseline, but not the top classifier

text: Lowest validation accuracy in this sweep
textPreview: Lowest validation accuracy in this sweep

text: Takeaway: ConvNeXt-Tiny is the strongest classifier, but this notebook did not report the same reject-option metrics as the CLIP + kNN notebooks.
textPreview: Takeaway: ConvNeXt-Tiny is the strongest classifier, but this notebook did not report the same reject-option metrics as the CLIP + kNN notebooks.

text: Use the best classifier for in-domain recognition and a distance-based rejector for the safety decision. The hybrid story matches what the notebooks actually support.
textPreview: Use the best classifier for in-domain recognition and a distance-based rejector for the safety decision. The hybrid story matches what the notebooks actually support.

Best classifier in
the sweep. Outputs
an ID/OOD score.

TRUST below tau. SLOW DOWN
near tau. ABSTAIN when

text: One required follow-up: rerun both branches on one shared train/val/test protocol before any deployment claim.
textPreview: One required follow-up: rerun both branches on one shared train/val/test protocol before any deployment claim.

text: What the final presentation should claim
textPreview: What the final presentation should claim

text: The notebooks point to a clear direction, with one caveat: unify the evaluation split before turning the project result into a deployment claim.
textPreview: The notebooks point to a clear direction, with one caveat: unify the evaluation split before turning the project result into a deployment claim.

text:
- ConvNeXt-Tiny was the strongest
supervised backbone at 0.9368 validation
accuracy.
- CLIP ViT-B/32 + kNN was the strongest
explicit OOD gate at 0.7412 AUROC.
- Temperature scaling improved ResNet
calibration from 0.1037 ECE to 0.0161.
- Confidence-only OOD scores were too
conservative to make a practical gate.
textPreview: - ConvNeXt-Tiny was the strongest | supervised backbone at 0.9368 validation | accuracy. | - CLIP ViT-B/32 + kNN was the strongest | explicit OOD gate at 0.7412 AUROC. | - Temperature scaling improved ResNet | calibratio

text:
- Rebuild one shared benchmark split and
rerun every method on it.
- Add night, construction, and corruption
shifts for a broader OOD story.
- Report latency and memory so the gate
can be judged as a real-time component.
- Integrate the thresholded gate into the
simulator and measure intervention
quality.
textPreview: - Rebuild one shared benchmark split and | rerun every method on it. | - Add night, construction, and corruption | shifts for a broader OOD story. | - Report latency and memory so the gate | can be judged as a real-time

text: Final recommendation for the deck: present ConvNeXt-Tiny as the best classifier, CLIP ViT-B/32 + kNN as the best reject-option OOD detector, and a unified evaluation protocol as the immediate next milestone.
textPreview: Final recommendation for the deck: present ConvNeXt-Tiny as the best classifier, CLIP ViT-B/32 + kNN as the best reject-option OOD detector, and a unified evaluation protocol as the immediate next milestone.
